images.alt:
ご指摘の投稿は、私個人としては「国民の皆さまが政治に対して意見を表明すること自体を否定する趣旨ではない」と受け止めています。

一方で、議員事務所や行政機関において、短時間に大量の同一内容のFAXやメールが集中すると、通常業務や他の方からの問い合わせ対応に支障が生じる場合があります。その点への問題提起であったと理解しています。

私は、国民投票法改正案に限らず、賛成・反対を問わず国民の皆さまが政治に関心を持ち、声を届けようとされることは大切だと考えています。

また、多くの方が同じテーマについて意見を寄せている場合には、その背景にどのような懸念や課題意識があるのかを丁寧に受け止めることも、政治に携わる者の重要な役割だと思っています。

したがって、「国民の声を受け止めること」と
「業務に支障を生じさせない形で意見を届けていただくこと」の両方が大切ではないかと考えています。

record.text: Unaware of that one.

Last thought: if AI is over-hyped, but has some good uses, then I'd like to hope the environmental impact is minimal because we wouldn't be using it for every god damn thing*

(*Exasperated tone towards silly market forces, not you.)
If the computer shows an error, it can be retrained, but is probably more likely to find something important in the same way having thousands of people looking at one image is more valuable.

することがある。しかし、それを特定の政府や組織全体の意図として断定するには、極めて強い証拠が必要だ。

君の「第四の波」の見方を借りれば、むしろ現在起きているのは、＜AIによって隠されていた事実がすべて暴露される時代＞というより、＜膨大な情報と膨大な疑惑が同時に噴出し、人々が何を信じるべきか判断しにくくなる時代＞なのかもしれない。

その環境では、「誰かが隠しているはずだ」という仮説を立てること以上に、「どの証拠がどこまで確かなのか」を検証する能力が重要になると思う。

君の認識している事を別の言葉や概念に置き換えるという観察は興味深い。実際、社会には常にタブーが存在する。歴史的にも、「宗教的禁忌」「政治的禁忌」「性に関する禁忌」「国家に関する禁忌」があり、人々は直接言わずに比喩や婉曲表現を用いてきた。

AI時代には、アルゴリズム、モデレーション、炎上リスクなどの要因も加わるので、その傾向は強まるかもしれない。

ただし慎重であるべき点
君は＜政府や軍が少女を大人の性の玩具にしていた＞という非常に重大な主張をしている。この種の主張は、公開された信頼できる証拠がなければ事実として扱うことはできない。

社会には確かに、性的虐待、搾取、組織的不正、隠蔽が存在

AIは、「文書を要約できる」「パターンを見つけられる」「矛盾を指摘できる」「仮説を生成できる」。しかし、「事実だった」「犯罪だった」「隠蔽があった」と確定するには、証拠、証言、捜査、司法手続きが必要になる。

「膨大なお喋りから本心を探る」は可能か

ある程度は可能だ。例えば大規模言語モデルは、大量の発言から「共通する不満」「繰り返し現れる主張」「隠れた価値観」「社会的な感情の変化」を推測できる。しかし、これは「傾向」を読むことであって、個々の人間や組織の真意を確定することではない。人間自身ですら、自分の本心を完全には理解していないことがあるからね。

第四の波と「言い換え文化」

- Firefox와 Mozilla는 대형 브라우저와 다른 틈새 브라우저·오픈소스 조직으로 유지되어 왔고, 성장은 대형 브라우저 모방보다 커뮤니티와의 협업에서 나왔음
- Mozilla 직원은 급여를 받는 작은 일부이며, 브라우저를 만들고 번역하고 버그를 고친 커뮤니티를 고객이나 팬이 아니라 동료로 대해야 함
- DAU 하락에 대한 대응으로 대형 브라우저 기능을 따라가거나 “스타트업처럼 생각하기”를 반복했지만, Firefox 사용자는 이미 기본 브라우저와 다른 경험을 찾아온 사람들임

Introduction: A zero-click account takeover (ATO) vulnerability represents the pinnacle of stealth attacks — it requires no user interaction, no phishing link, no…

external.description: Introduction: A zero-click account takeover (ATO) vulnerability represents the pinnacle of stealth attacks — it requires no user interaction, no phishing link, no malicious app installation. Security researcher Maher Azzouzi recently disclosed such a flaw in Instagram’s authentication mechanism, where merely knowing a target’s username enables complete account compromise. With Meta failing to respond for three months despite a proof-of-concept (PoC) video, this exposure highlights critical gaps in social media platform security and bug bounty response protocols.

I'd like to make everyone aware of a rule change put in place regarding rule 16. The new rule reads as follows:

No AI/LLM-generated content.

This includes (but is not limited to) images, text, code, and research supplemented by an AI or LLM. If an AI/LLM was involved, do not share it in the Discord or any of our websites.

What this means: 

Do not post any AI generated art, AI generated music, text prompts from ChatGPT/Claude/Copilot/Google Gemini/whatnot, AI generated code or any other forms of AI generated content.
Do not use AI for research purposes, especially involving datamining games, disassembly/decompilation projects, searching for cut and unused video game content or any sort of upscaling/reconstructing content.
If your project involves AI in any way, we do not want to see it either here nor on TCRF/Data Crystal.

I hope this clears up any remaining uncertainty regarding our position on using AI.

images.alt:
Yesterday I took a short trip. I began with a ride on the local Hyperloop, which ran through a tunnel dug by Boring Company. Then I used my neural implant to summon a fully self-driving Tesla robotaxi. While enroute I read the latest news from
the Mars colony.
OK, none of that actually happened, because those products don't exist. There are no working Hyperloops. The Boring Company has not dug any commercial tunnels. Tesla has a few self-driving — though not fully self-driving - taxis in Austin and nowhere else. (Google's Waymo driverless taxis are operational in several major hubs.) Neuralink, which is purportedly pioneering brain implants, has tested its products in a handful of patients but done no more than that. And of course there is no Mars colony: there have been no manned flights to Mars, nor the prospect of any for the foreseeable future.
Yet at various points over the past decade Elon Musk promised that each of these services would be available by 2025 if not sooner.

->POLITICO | More on "AI models threatening financial system" at BigEarthData.ai | #AI

external.description: BRUSSELS — Advanced artificial intelligence models such as Anthropic’s Mythos “can be used to destroy the financial system” if adopted by bad actors, the head of the International Monetary Fund has warned. Finance watchdogs have been on high alert over the risks from superhacking AI models since April, when Anthropic announced its powerful new model Mythos, which has advanced cyber capabilities and triggered fears that it could be used for devastating hacks of critical systems. “What we recognize is that Mythos is just the beginning, there will be more like it,” Kristalina Georgieva told journalists in Brussels. Georgieva said frontier AI models can be used positively to identify "cybersecurity vulnerabilities with speed and scale that was unthinkable until now,” which could be used to "patch these vulnerabilities and protect the financial system from attacks." “But in the wrong hands, that same capacity can be used to destroy the financial system and this is something that puts us in a position of catching up,” she added. In response, countries should “have all the elements of cybersecurity in place,” Georgieva said, calling for “more cooperation” between rich and poor countries and between the public and private sector to prepare for the risks...
external.title: Advanced AI models can 'destroy the financial system,' IMF boss warns

value.text:
About a half-dozen of the writers I work with have confessed to feeling paranoid about their use of em-dashes (the long ones: "—") because they fear people will believe their stuff was written by an AI.

To which I say: Pfffft. Use as many dang dashes as you want. And let your human voice sing out.
